question: Does the output generate exactly 3 distinct creative directions for the marketing campaign?
Does each of the 3 creative directions include exactly one slogan?
Does each of the 3 creative directions include exactly one Key Visual (KV) image?
Does the output explicitly select one "best" direction to extend after presenting the initial options?
Does the output generate exactly 3 social media posters for the selected best direction?
Are the three proposed creative directions conceptually distinct (e.g., focusing on different angles like emotion, function, or lifestyle) rather than just minor visual variations?
Does the text content explicitly align with the "Urban Light Outdoor" brand tone (e.g., accessible nature, city-adjacent) and the "Spring" season constraints?
Is the text structured professionally (e.g., using headers, clear sections) and does it use appropriate marketing terminology?
Are the generated slogans concise, memorable, and suitable for an advertising campaign?
Do the Key Visual (KV) images visually depict an "Urban Light Outdoor" aesthetic (e.g., city parks, glamping, rooftop camping) with clear Spring season cues?
Are the generated images of high aesthetic quality (professional composition, lighting) and free from severe AI distortions or artifacts?
Does each generated KV image strictly correspond to the specific theme and details described in its adjacent text?
Are the images placed immediately adjacent to their corresponding creative direction descriptions or poster concepts to facilitate easy reference?
Does the text naturally introduce and reference the generated images (e.g., "The KV below shows...") to ensure a smooth interleaved reading experience?
Do the 3 extended social media posters maintain a consistent visual identity (color palette, style, design elements) derived from the selected creative direction?
Do the 3 poster images visually align with the specific content themes described for them in the text (e.g., product shot vs lifestyle shot vs call-to-action)?
Do the 3 extended social media posters vary in content focus (e.g., distinct scenarios or objectives) rather than repeating the exact same image composition?
